candidates.text:
"Subject: Let's chat about what really gets under my skin

Hey,

I've been thinking a lot lately about what really triggers me, and I wanted to share it with you. It's not just the big stuff—sometimes it's the little things that really get under my skin. For instance, when people interrupt me mid-sentence, I feel like my thoughts are being dismissed, and that can really throw me off. Also, when plans change at the last minute, it can feel like my time isn't respected, and that can be really frustrating.

Another thing that really bothers me is when people are passive-aggressive. It's like they're saying one thing but meaning another, and it's so hard to know how to respond. And don't get me started on people who are overly critical without offering any constructive feedback. It feels like they're just trying to tear you down.

I know these things might seem small, but they can really add up and make me feel overwhelmed. I'm trying to be more aware of these triggers and work on them, but it's not always easy.

Anyway, I wanted to share this with you because I value our friendship and I want to be open and honest with you. I hope you can understand where I'm coming from.

Let's catch up soon!

Best,
[Your Name]"

"Hi there,

I hope you're doing well. I wanted to share something that's been on my mind lately. Lately, I've been feeling more triggered than usual by certain situations. One thing that really gets to me is when people interrupt me while I'm speaking. It makes me feel like my thoughts aren't valued, and it can be really frustrating. Another trigger for me is when plans change at the last minute without much notice. It throws off my sense of control and can make me feel anxious. I'm working on managing these triggers, but it's not always easy. I'd love to hear if you have any similar experiences or if you have any advice on how to deal with them.

Take care,  
[Your Name]"

"Hey, I’ve been doing some self-reflection lately and wanted to share what really gets under my skin. It’s not just big things, but small, everyday triggers that catch me off guard. One major one is when someone interrupts me mid-sentence—it makes me feel like my thoughts aren’t valued. Another is when plans change last minute without much notice; I need a little breathing room to adjust. I also get really frustrated when people use passive-aggressive comments instead of being direct. And honestly, cluttered spaces can make me feel anxious and scattered. Sharing this helps me understand myself better, and maybe it’ll help us communicate more clearly too. Let me know if any of this resonates with you!"

"Hey! I’ve been doing some thinking lately about what really sets me off, and I wanted to share a bit with you. Honestly, my biggest triggers are usually things like being interrupted when I’m trying to focus on a task, or when someone makes a sarcastic joke at my expense. It’s also hard for me when plans change last minute without much notice—I get really stressed by uncertainty. I’m working on communicating this better so I don’t just react in the moment. It’s interesting to reflect on these things, and I hope sharing this helps you understand me a bit better. Let me know what resonates with you! Talk soon."
